product_overview: A production-grade, scan-first URL shortener backend that provides authenticated URL creation and management, public redirects with Redis caching and rate limiting, asynchronous analytics and QR generation via BullMQ workers, and system/worker monitoring endpoints.
core_goals: Enable authenticated users to create, manage, and soft-delete short links with pre-creation malware scanning and rate limiting.
Provide a high-performance public redirect endpoint with Redis caching and async analytics offload.
Capture and expose click analytics (total, daily breakdowns, country and device distributions) to link owners.
Support lifecycle operations (disable/enable/extend) with cache invalidation and soft deletes.
Run background workers (scan, creation, QR, analytics, maintenance, logging) with internal triggers and monitoring endpoints.
Expose health and metrics endpoints for operational visibility and safe internal worker control.

features.name: Authentication
features.description: Register, authenticate, inspect current session and logout using JWT-based sessions backed by server-side sessions.
user_flows: POST /api/auth/register with { email, password } -> Receive 201 with token and user object
POST /api/auth/login with { email, password } -> Receive 200 with token and user object
GET /api/auth/me with Authorization: Bearer <token> -> Receive 200 with user info
POST /api/auth/logout with Authorization: Bearer <token> -> Receive 200 { message: 'Logged out successfully' }
POST /api/auth/login with invalid password -> Receive 401 Unauthorized
POST /api/auth/register with already-registered email -> Receive 400 Validation error

features.name: URL Management API
features.description: Authenticated CRUD for short URLs: create (scan-first pipeline + fan-out), list (paginated), fetch and soft-delete; creation enforces rate limits and scanning.
user_flows: POST /api/auth/login with valid credentials -> Receive 200 with JWT token
POST /api/urls with Authorization: Bearer <token> and body { longUrl, customAlias?, expiryDate? } -> Receive 201 with url object (shortUrl, shortCode, urlId) and remaining creation quota
GET /api/urls with Authorization: Bearer <token>?page=1&limit=20 -> Receive 200 with paginated urls array
DELETE /api/urls/:id with Authorization: Bearer <token> -> Receive 200 { message: 'URL deleted successfully' }
POST /api/urls without Authorization header -> Receive 401 Unauthorized
POST /api/urls with Authorization: Bearer <token> and body containing a URL flagged malicious by scan -> Receive 400 Validation error (URL rejected due to malware)
POST /api/urls with Authorization but after user exceeded daily creation limit -> Receive 429 Rate limit exceeded

features.name: URL Redirection
features.description: Public redirect endpoint resolving short codes to original URLs with Redis caching, redirect-level rate limiting, validation (status/expiry/max clicks), and async analytics logging.
user_flows: GET /abc123 (public short code) without Authorization -> Receive 302 Redirect to https://example.com (happy path, may populate Redis cache)
GET /abc123 again (public short code) -> Receive 302 Redirect to https://example.com (served from cache on subsequent request)
GET /nonexistentcode -> Receive 302 -> /not-found or 404 / 'URL not found' (public not-found handling)
GET /expiredcode -> Receive 410 Link expired (expired or inactive link)
Send many GET /abc123 from same IP until limit exceeded -> Receive 429 Rate limit exceeded

features.name: Analytics API
features.description: Authenticated endpoints returning click analytics: total clicks, daily breakdown (date range filter), country and device distributions for a specific URL owned by the caller.
user_flows: POST /api/auth/login with valid credentials -> Receive 200 with JWT token
GET /api/urls/:id/analytics with Authorization: Bearer <token> -> Receive 200 { total_clicks: number }
GET /api/urls/:id/analytics/daily?startDate=2026-03-01&endDate=2026-03-31 with Authorization: Bearer <token> -> Receive 200 Array<{ date, clicks }>
GET /api/urls/:id/analytics/countries with Authorization: Bearer <token> -> Receive 200 Array<{ country, clicks }>
GET /api/urls/:id/analytics/devices with Authorization: Bearer <token> -> Receive 200 Array<{ device, clicks }>
GET /api/urls/:id/analytics without Authorization -> Receive 401 Unauthorized
GET /api/urls/:id/analytics/daily?startDate=2026-04-10&endDate=2026-04-01 with Authorization: Bearer <token> -> Receive 400 Invalid date range

features.name: Lifecycle Management API
features.description: Endpoints to check link status, disable or enable a link temporarily, and extend expiry; operations invalidate Redis cache where applicable.
user_flows: POST /api/auth/login with valid credentials -> Receive 200 with JWT token
GET /api/urls/:id/status with Authorization: Bearer <token> -> Receive 200 { status, expires_at }
PATCH /api/urls/:id/disable with Authorization: Bearer <token> -> Receive 200 { message: 'URL disabled' }
GET /api/urls/:id/status with Authorization: Bearer <token> -> Receive 200 { status: 'disabled', expires_at }
PATCH /api/urls/:id/enable with Authorization: Bearer <token> -> Receive 200 { message: 'URL enabled' }
PATCH /api/urls/:id/extend with Authorization: Bearer <token> and body { expiry_date } -> Receive 200 { message: 'URL expiration extended' }
PATCH /api/urls/:id/disable without Authorization -> Receive 401 Unauthorized
PATCH /api/urls/:id/extend with invalid expiry_date format -> Receive 400 Validation error
PATCH /api/urls/:id/disable for non-existent id with Authorization -> Receive 404 URL not found

features.name: QR Code API
features.description: Return QR code image URL for a short link; QR generation is performed asynchronously by a background worker, so QR may be absent immediately after creation.
user_flows: POST /api/auth/login with valid credentials -> Receive 200 with JWT token
POST /api/urls with Authorization: Bearer <token> and body { longUrl } -> Receive 201 with url object (triggers qrQueue job)
GET /api/urls/:id/qrcode with Authorization: Bearer <token> -> Receive 200 { qr_code: 'https://ik.imagekit.io/.../qr_<id>.png' } (happy path after worker finished)
GET /api/urls/:id/qrcode with Authorization: Bearer <token> immediately after creation (QR still generating) -> Receive 404 QR code not found (still generating)
GET /api/urls/:id/qrcode without Authorization -> Receive 401 Unauthorized

features.name: System Monitoring API
features.description: Public health checks and metrics plus an authenticated endpoint to show remaining rate-limit quotas for the current user.
user_flows: GET /api/health -> Receive 200 { database: 'healthy', redis: 'healthy', workers: 'running' }
GET /api/metrics -> Receive 200 { total_urls, total_clicks, redis_memory }
POST /api/auth/login with valid credentials -> Receive 200 with JWT token
GET /api/rate-limit with Authorization: Bearer <token> -> Receive 200 { creations_remaining, redirects_remaining }
GET /api/health when a dependency is down -> Receive 503 Unhealthy status with error details
GET /api/rate-limit without Authorization -> Receive 401 Unauthorized

features.name: Internal Worker API
features.description: Internal endpoints to trigger maintenance/background jobs (dead link scan, click sync, malware scan) and to inspect queue status; protected by x-internal-secret header or development-mode bypass.
user_flows: POST /api/internal/workers/deadlink-scan with header x-internal-secret: <secret> -> Receive 200 { message: 'Dead link scan job enqueued' }
POST /api/internal/workers/sync-clicks with header x-internal-secret: <secret> -> Receive 200 { message: 'Click sync job enqueued' }
POST /api/internal/workers/scan-malware with header x-internal-secret: <secret> and body { url_id, long_url } -> Receive 200 { message: 'Malware scan job enqueued' }
GET /api/internal/queues with header x-internal-secret: <secret> -> Receive 200 { queues: { scan, creation, qr, analytics, maintenance, logging }, active_workers, worker_detail }
POST /api/internal/workers/deadlink-scan without x-internal-secret (production) -> Receive 401 Unauthorized
POST /api/internal/workers/scan-malware with missing url_id in body -> Receive 400 Validation error

features.name: Redis Cache Layer
features.description: Redis-backed caching for redirects, click counters, and rate limiting; cache entries and counters are manipulated transparently by public and internal APIs and background workers.
user_flows: GET /abc123 (first request) -> Receive 302 Redirect to long_url (cache miss, DB lookup, then cache short:abc123 set)
GET /abc123 (subsequent request) -> Receive 302 Redirect to long_url (served from Redis cache)
PATCH /api/urls/:id/disable with Authorization: Bearer <token> -> Receive 200 { message: 'URL disabled' } (handler invalidates Redis short:{code})
GET /abc123 after disable -> Receive 410 Link expired or 302 to /link-expired (cache invalidated and link considered disabled)
POST /api/internal/workers/sync-clicks with header x-internal-secret: <secret> -> Receive 200 { message: 'Click sync job enqueued' } (flushes Redis clicks:{code} counters to DB)
GET /api/health when Redis is down -> Receive 503 with redis unhealthy

features.name: Background Workers
features.description: BullMQ-based workers handle scanning, link creation, QR generation, analytics logging, maintenance (dead link checks, expiry), and click sync; workers are observable through internal endpoints and system health.
user_flows: POST /api/auth/login with valid credentials -> Receive 200 with JWT token

POST /api/urls with Authorization: Bearer <token> and body { longUrl } -> Receive 201 with url object (scanQueue + linkCreationQueue used synchronously, qrQueue and analyticsQueue enqueued asynchronously)
GET /api/urls/:id/qrcode with Authorization: Bearer <token> after worker runs -> Receive 200 with qr_code URL (qrWorker produced QR and stored URL)
GET /api/health when workers are not running -> Receive 503 Unhealthy status with workers error (known limitation: worker status may be static if not fixed)
POST /api/internal/workers/sync-clicks with header x-internal-secret: <secret> -> Receive 200 { message: 'Click sync job enqueued' }
POST /api/internal/workers/deadlink-scan with invalid internal secret -> Receive 401 Unauthorized

known_limitations.issue: Health check returns static 'running' for workers instead of dynamic check
Auth type says NONE in testsprite_config.json despite JWT auth being implemented
known_limitations.impact: Test configuration may not reflect actual auth requirements

known_limitations.issue: Internal API uses x-internal-secret header OR NODE_ENV=development bypass
known_limitations.impact: Internal endpoints accessible without auth in development mode
